methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nox Metals is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zane Hengsperger that has raised $5.1M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of one public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers in robotics and manufacturing, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable valuation approach here. Using total capital raised ($5.1M) as a proxy for current fair value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, the analysis implies a valuation of $13M. The Peer-Median method was not deployable due to insufficient narrow peer data—while 11 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a sector-specific median unreliable.

The triangulated valuation range is $11M (low) to $15M (high), with a median of $13M. This range reflects the single applicable method and carries material uncertainty given the limited quantitative anchoring. The key limitation is the absence of sufficiently comparable Seed-stage robotics companies with public valuations or recent funding data. Refinement would require either identifying additional narrow-stage peers or obtaining more detailed financial or traction metrics for the existing comp set.
